description.en: World-renowned red wine from the Bordeaux region, known for its complex blends of Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, and Cabernet Franc
The world's most famous sparkling wine, produced exclusively in the Champagne region using the traditional methode champenoise
Anise-flavored aperitif from Provence, traditionally diluted with cold water, turning from amber to milky white
Traditional sparkling cider from Normandy and Brittany, made from heritage apple varieties and served in ceramic bowls
Prestigious double-distilled grape brandy from the Cognac region, aged in Limousin oak barrels and classified by age
Classic French aperitif combining creme de cassis with white Burgundy wine or Champagne
Premium apple brandy from Normandy, distilled from cider and aged in oak barrels
The cornerstone of French cafe culture, from the morning espresso at the zinc bar to the leisurely cafe creme on a terrace
